comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@
 ! Good: example.org###rek; ||example.com/ads/
 ! Bad: example.org#@##banner_ad (for instance, should be in allowlist.txt or in antiadblock.txt)
 !
+brokker.news##.container-bn
comment.body:
Thanks for the detailed review! Before I apply the split, I wanted to check one alternative. While testing, I noticed that the proposed rules don't seem to cover two ad-related cases: - the affiliate "Купити криптовалюту" buttons (.btn-ads -> link.brokker.news/exchange);
Screenshots
- the mobile header banner, where an empty .container-bn-mb remains after blocking.
Screenshots
Would something like this be acceptable instead? 
brokker.news##.container-bn:has(ins[class="adsbygoogle"], .btn-ads)
Would this be a reasonable compromise? It should still limit the rule to ad-related containers while covering the affiliate buttons and the mobile leftover.

labels.description: The issue was not processed on time and was bumped to the top of the list

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://filmix.my/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffilmix.my%2F) ### Comment > On Filmix.my and other Filmix mirror sites, a filter from the 'Teasers' group blocks helpful pop-up notifications (see screenshot 1) when you click the 'Save Watch Time' button. comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@
 ! Good: example.org###rek; ||example.com/ads/
 ! Bad: example.org#@##banner_ad (for instance, should be in allowlist.txt or in antiadblock.txt)
 !
+brokker.news##.container-bn
comment.body:
> the affiliate "Купити криптовалюту" buttons (.btn-ads -> link.brokker.news/exchange);

Good catch! I didn't realize I still had the remaining ad button. But a specific rule should target the same elements. Because each element could have different roles and behaviors even in the future.

And you should narrow these rules using the `.post-content` article container. It's important.

> the mobile header banner, where an empty .container-bn-mb remains after blocking.

You can continue block with the `+` next-sibling combinator.

```adblock
brokker.news###header + .container-bn-header + .container-bn-mb
````

The conclusion is:

```suggestion
brokker.news###header + .container-bn-header
brokker.news###header + .container-bn-header + .container-bn-mb
brokker.news##.post-content .container-bn:has(ins[class="adsbygoogle"])
brokker.news##.post-content .container-bn:has(.btn-ads)
```

comment.body:
> the inline trigger seems to be rendered only on the first visit > In a private window, the initial page source contains:
(Screenshots) Got it. I can find this inline script in a private window 👍🏻
Please keep using `abort-current-inline-script` and don't forget the `#` prefix. And apply my suggestion using the "Commit suggestion" button. It is important to auto-resolve this conversation. ```suggestion brokker.news#%#//scriptlet('abort-current-inline-script', '$', '#modalNewsletter') ```

issue.title: Fix #232032 brokker.news
issue.body:
## Prerequisites - [x] This is not an ad/bug report; - [x] My code follows the [guidelines](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and [syntax](https://kb.adguard.com/general/how-to-create-your-own-ad-filters) of this project; - [x] I have performed a self-review of my own changes; - [x] My changes do not break web sites, apps and files structure. ## What problem does the pull request fix? - [x] Missed ads or ad leftovers; - [ ] Website or app doesn't work properly; - [ ] AdGuard gets detected on a website; - [ ] Missed analytics or tracker; - [ ] Social media buttons — share, like, tweet, etc; - [x] Annoyances — pop-ups, cookie warnings, etc; - [ ] Filters maintenance. ## What issue is being fixed? Fix https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/232032 ### Add your comment Addresses two reports on brokker.news. Ads / ad leftovers Added: brokker.news##.container-bn The site uses .container-bn as a common wrapper for both desktop and mobile ad blocks. After the ad content is blocked, empty containers remain visible. The same wrapper is also used for affiliate exchange banners, so a single rule covers all reported cases. Newsletter popup Added: brokker.news#%#//scriptlet('abort-current-inline-script', '$', 'modalNewsletter') The newsletter modal is automatically opened on page load. A scriptlet was used instead of a cosmetic hide rule to prevent the modal from being initialized in the first place and avoid page scroll issues caused by Bootstrap's modal handling. The reported "Слідкуйте за нами" section was left unchanged, as it contains only regular links to the site's social media pages.
Screenshots before: after:
### Terms - [x] By submitting this issue, I agree that pull request does not contain private info and all conditions are met

comment.body: Thanks, I missed the existing Social Filter coverage pattern and incorrectly assumed this widget was out of scope because it only contained links to the site's own social pages.

comment.body:
I guess that in theory it should be possible to replicate blocked popup by running following script in a browser console:
```js
window.open('https://click.linksynergy.com/fs-bin/click?id=pLhf5imLITg&offerid=200892.10001086&type=3&subid=0')
```
But I cannot reproduce it even if I add `badfilter` to exception.
I was able to reproduce blocked popup only with French filter enabled, but there is `/click?*&subid=$popup` rule, so it's expected.

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

New change of tinyShield's build script for Apple Safari Extension user is available.

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://www.tarjetaciudadanafuengirola.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tarjetaciudadanafuengirola.com%2F)

### Comment
> Hello,
> 
> This domain hosts the official mobile application of the Ayuntamiento de Fuengirola
> (Fuengirola City Council, Málaga, Spain), a Spanish public administration service.
> 
> The domain is currently being blocked by AdGuard DNS, preventing legitimate citizens
> from accessing public services such as: municipal identification card management,
> appointment booking with city services, sports center access, library services and
> other digital services provided by the City Council.
> 
> Evidence this is a legitimate service, not malicious:
> 
> 1. The website serves a valid SSL certificate issued by AWS.
> 
> 2. The application is officially commissioned by the Ayuntamiento de Fuengirola
>    (https://www.fuengirola.es), with public references on their official website.
> 
> 3. VirusTotal shows no issues.
> 
> 4. The service has been operational for several years without any reported
>    security incident.
> 
> 5. The technical provider is WebDreams SL, a Spanish software company specialized
>    in citizen services for Spanish municipalities, also providing similar services
>    to other Spanish cities (Getafe, Redondela, Lalín, Marratxí, Ciudad Real,
>    Puertollano, among others), all of which work correctly.
> 
> Could you please review and remove this domain from your blocklist?
> 
> Thank you for your time.

```
Username: @wdsistemas
```


### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/1wftpkjwj2josooc0cwkgc8o0gogwosc9tkpbgnz9c.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/268xlvbxfxq88g84ogokoc0gk00ow84ermdgk3d2x4.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default
System version: | `Android 16`
Browser: | Chrome

comment.body:
> VirusTotal shows no issues

I see some detections [there](https://www.virustotal.com/gui/domain/tarjetaciudadanafuengirola.com?nocache=1), but it might be a false positive.
Anyway, it's been removed from our base. Update should be available today.

> The domain is currently being blocked by AdGuard DNS, preventing legitimate citizens
> from accessing public services such as: municipal identification card management,
> appointment booking with city services, sports center access, library services and

> Evidence this is a legitimate service, not malicious:
>

>

>    (https://www.fuengirola.es), with public references on their official website.

> 
> 4. The service has been operational for several years without any reported
>    security incident.
> 
> 5. The technical provider is WebDreams SL, a Spanish software company specialized
>    in citizen services for Spanish municipalities, also providing similar services
>    to other Spanish cities (Getafe, Redondela, Lalín, Marratxí, Ciudad Real,
>    Puertollano, among others), all of which work correctly.

> Could you please review and remove this domain from your blocklist?

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

Desktop

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter

### Add your comment and screenshots

2. Lamy.com was reported before, but is still getting blocked. They're a German stationery brand.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script)
[https://mailchi.mp/e831cd3fd520/dubnov-novinky-fondu-17997828?e=5023164aa3](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmailchi.mp%2Fe831cd3fd520%2Fdubnov-novinky-fondu-17997828%3Fe%3D5023164aa3)

### Comment
> The URL is blocked when using public AdGuard DNS (dns.adguard.com) on Android Private DNS.
> 
> When Private DNS is switched to Automatic, the page opens correctly.
> 
> This appears to be a false positive. The URL belongs to a legitimate Mailchimp newsletter.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script)
[https://mailchi.mp/e831cd3fd520/dubnov-novinky-fondu-17997828?e=5023164aa3](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmailchi.mp%2Fe831cd3fd520%2Fdubnov-novinky-fondu-17997828%3Fe%3D5023164aa3)

### Comment
> The URL is blocked when using public AdGuard DNS (dns.adguard.com) on Android Private DNS.
> 
> When Private DNS is switched to Automatic, the page opens correctly.
> 
> This appears to be a false positive. The URL belongs to a legitimate Mailchimp newsletter.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/pkugwpgradc4w4sgk0kswks8gcwwk80e3yyutbzqug.jpg?nc=1)

</details>

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Router
DNS server: | Default
System version: | `Android 16`
Browser: | Chrome

### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://m.arabseed.show/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fm.arabseed.show%2F) ### Comment > I am using the paid version of AdGuard on iPadOS. > > Many websites are able to detect AdGuard and display anti-adblock warnings. Some websites block video servers, disable content, or require me to disable AdGuard before I can use the site. > > I would like AdGuard to improve anti-adblock protection and detection bypass mechanisms across websites in general, not only on a single website. > > I also want stronger protection against advertisements, cookie notices, pop-ups, redirects, tracking scripts, and other annoyances while keeping websites fully functional. > > Please continue improving filter lists and anti-adblock rules so websites cannot detect AdGuard or force users to disable it. > > Thank you. ### Screenshots

comment.body:
I would like to add another note:

The issue is not limited to a single website. It occurs on many streaming websites on both iOS and Android.

Some of the problems I experience include:

* Anti-adblock warnings asking me to disable AdGuard.
* Advertisements not being completely blocked.
* Advertisement breaks or advertising pages appearing on some websites.
* Pop-ups and unwanted redirects.
* Some streaming servers or video players failing to work.
* Certain video qualities or sources not loading correctly.
* Slow website performance or video playback interruptions.

I would appreciate continued improvements to anti-adblock protection and filtering rules across different websites and applications.

Thank you.

issue.title: m.arabseed.show
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://m.arabseed.show/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fm.arabseed.show%2F) ### Comment > I am using the paid version of AdGuard on iPadOS. > > Many websites are able to detect AdGuard and display anti-adblock warnings. Some websites block video servers, disable content, or require me to disable AdGuard before I can use the site. > > I would like AdGuard to improve anti-adblock protection and detection bypass mechanisms across websites in general, not only on a single website. > > I also want stronger protection against advertisements, cookie notices, pop-ups, redirects, tracking scripts, and other annoyances while keeping websites fully functional. > > Please continue improving filter lists and anti-adblock rules so websites cannot detect AdGuard or force users to disable it. > > Thank you. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/54ei5tihju8skkg80wwoskkg88kwocwo7clm9qwclc.jpg?nc=1)

comment.body:
This issue is not limited to m.arabseed.show.

Many websites can detect AdGuard and display anti-adblock messages, block video playback, or require disabling AdGuard before accessing content.

The problem affects multiple websites, not just one specific domain.

Please improve anti-adblock detection bypass rules across websites in general.

Thank you.

Some websites block video servers, disable content, or require me to disable AdGuard before I can use the site. > > I would like AdGuard to improve anti-adblock protection and detection bypass mechanisms across websites in general, not only on a single website. > > I also want stronger protection against advertisements, cookie notices, pop-ups, redirects, tracking scripts, and other annoyances while keeping websites fully functional. > > Please continue improving filter lists and anti-adblock rules so websites cannot detect AdGuard or force users to disable it. > > Thank you. ### Screenshots
